name: A.A.E.C.
description_short: Arizona Agribusiness & Equine Center (AAEC) is a network of early college high schools offering tuition-free dual enrollment and career and technical education (CTE) programs across Arizona.
description_long: Arizona Agribusiness & Equine Center (AAEC) operates as a public charter school system with multiple campuses across Arizona, including locations in South Mountain, Paradise Valley, Mesa, Prescott Valley, and Estrella Mountain, plus an online program. The organization's primary mission is to prepare young adults for excellence in academics, social responsibility, industry employability, and lifelong learning through specialized education pathways. AAEC offers early college high school programs that allow students to earn college credits while still in high school, all tuition-free, which significantly reduces the cost burden of higher education for enrolled families. The school is accredited as an A+ School and emphasizes Career and Technical Education (CTE) across multiple industry sectors, including its namesake agribusiness and equine programs. What distinguishes AAEC is its dual-enrollment model that allows students to simultaneously complete high school requirements and begin college coursework at no direct cost to families, combined with industry-focused training that emphasizes employability outcomes. The organization maintains transparent reporting of teacher salaries (averaging $55,567 in FY 2026) and engages with community programs like the Congressional App Challenge and FFA. AAEC is fundamentally an educational institution, not a consumer finance company. It provides no lending services, credit products, financial assistance programs, or consumer financial services of any kind. This organization was incorrectly categorized as a payday alternative lender and should be removed from consumer finance databases entirely, as it operates exclusively in the K-12 and early college education space.
services: Early college high school programs with tuition-free dual enrollment
Career and Technical Education (CTE) instruction across multiple industry sectors
Agribusiness and equine center specialization programs
A+ School accredited academics and college preparation
Online high school education option (AAEC Online)
Student email and learning management system access (Canvas)
Parent and student information portal (Parent/Student VUE)
Scholarship opportunities (Dr. Roberts Legacy Scholarship Donation program)
Tax credit donation programs for educational support
FFA (Future Farmers of America) program participation
Campus locations across five Arizona regions with varying program focus
School calendar and event scheduling for enrolled families
pros: Tuition-free early college program allows students to earn college credits before high school graduation with zero cost
Multiple campus locations across Arizona provide geographic accessibility (South Mountain, Paradise Valley, Mesa, Prescott Valley, Estrella Mountain, plus online)
Industry-specific CTE programs in agribusiness and equine fields provide specialized career training and employability focus
A+ School accreditation demonstrates academic quality and accountability standards
Transparent institutional reporting including average teacher salaries ($55,567 FY 2026) shows financial accountability
cons: Not a consumer finance company — provides no lending, credit, or financial products whatsoever
Serves only high school students ages 14-18, not general adult consumers
Early college enrollment requires meeting academic prerequisites and application criteria; not universally available to all
Geographic limitation to Arizona locations only; no national or online-only options for out-of-state students
Specialized focus on agribusiness and equine careers may not align with all student interests or career goals
best_for: Arizona high school students seeking tuition-free dual enrollment and college credit before graduation
Families wanting to reduce college costs through early college programs without education debt
Students interested in career and technical education in agribusiness, equine, or skilled trades
diagnosis: AAEC is a public charter school system providing free early college and career education to Arizona high school students—not a consumer finance provider. This organization should be immediately removed from CreditDoc's consumer finance database as it provides no lending, credit products, or financial services of any kind and serves exclusively as an educational institution.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
